---
title: "SPC Monitoring Dashboard"
author: "Sharon Jia Yi Lee"
date: "2026-05-17"
output: html_document
---

## Overview
Statistical Process Control (SPC) analysis across 5 process parameters 
and 5 fabrication steps using real semiconductor sensor data. 
Shewhart X-bar control charts are applied to detect out-of-control signals 
that could indicate quality issues in wafer production.

## Dataset
- **Source:** Semiconductor Wafer Defect Classification Dataset (Kaggle)
- **Records:** 5,000 wafer sensor readings
- **Parameters monitored:** Temperature, Pressure, Gas Flow, Etch Rate, Voltage, Current
- **Process Steps:** CMP, Deposition, Etching, Lithography, Oxidation

## Violations Summary Table
```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(qcc)
library(ggplot2)

df <- read.csv("semiconductor_wafer_defect_dataset.csv")

parameters <- c("temperature_c", "pressure_torr", "etch_rate_nm_min", 
                "voltage_v", "current_ma")
steps <- unique(df$process_step)
results <- data.frame()

for (param in parameters) {
  for (step in steps) {
    step_data <- df %>% filter(process_step == step) %>% pull(!!sym(param))
    n_groups  <- floor(length(step_data) / 5)
    grouped   <- matrix(step_data[1:(n_groups * 5)], ncol=5, byrow=TRUE)
    chart     <- qcc(grouped, type="xbar", plot=FALSE)
    beyond    <- sum(chart$violations$beyond.limits)
    runs      <- length(chart$violations$violating.runs)
    results   <- rbind(results, data.frame(
      Parameter      = param,
      Process_Step   = step,
      UCL            = round(chart$limits[,2][1], 3),
      LCL            = round(chart$limits[,1][1], 3),
      Beyond_Limits  = beyond,
      Violating_Runs = runs,
      Status         = ifelse(beyond > 0 | runs > 0, "âš  OUT OF CONTROL", "âś“ Stable")
    ))
  }
}

knitr::kable(results)
```

## SPC Violations Heatmap
```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
results$Total_Violations <- results$Beyond_Limits + results$Violating_Runs

ggplot(results, aes(x=Process_Step, y=Parameter, fill=Total_Violations)) +
  geom_tile(color="white", linewidth=0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label=ifelse(Total_Violations > 0,
                             paste0("âš  ", Total_Violations), "âś“")),
            size=3.5, color="white", fontface="bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low="steelblue", high="tomato", name="Total\nViolations") +
  labs(
    title    = "SPC Violations Heatmap â€” Wafer Fabrication Process",
    subtitle = "Red = high violations | Blue = stable | âś“ = in control",
    x        = "Process Step",
    y        = "Parameter"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title   = element_text(face="bold", size=14),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1)
  )
```

## Key Findings
- **22 of 25** parameter-step combinations flagged as out of control
- **pressure_torr in CMP** has the highest violations (190) â€” highest priority for investigation
- **temperature_c in Lithography and Oxidation** show critical beyond-limit breaches (187, 157)
- **3 stable combinations:** temperature Deposition, etch rate Lithography, voltage Oxidation

## Methodology
- Shewhart X-bar control charts with subgroup size n=5
- Western Electric Rules applied for run detection
- Data balanced using SMOTE prior to defect classification
